title: 4-file commit: PASSWORD_REGEX tightened, sessionId key added, paymentError field added, CLEARTOAST action added
commit_message: feat: tighten password validation, add session tracking, payment error state, and toast clear action

why_maximum_blast.explanation: All 4 changed files appear across 5 different test suites. constants.ts and storage.ts share sources with both standalone and cross-dependent suites. paymentReducer shares sources with cross-dependent. toastReducer shares sources with feature/favourites. Result: 47/77 tests selected across ALL 3 categories.

constants.ts → PASSWORD_REGEX: HIGH    → standalone/regex.constants.test.js  (all other regex constants — sibling in same file)

CRITICAL.reason: describe() label or symbols[] directly matches a changed symbol name

tests.test: PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD > accepts a password meeting all requirements
tests.why: describe label contains PASSWORD_REGEX; sources = constants.ts (changed); NOTE: PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD is a separate symbol but shares the same file — sister-symbol escalation

tests.test: PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD > rejects a password without an uppercase letter
PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD > rejects a password without a special character
PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD > rejects a password longer than 30 characters
isUserLoggedIn reads storageKeys.token > returns false when no token is stored
tests.why: symbols = [isUserLoggedIn, storageKeys]; storageKeys is a changed symbol (new key added)

tests.test: isUserLoggedIn reads storageKeys.token > returns true after a token has been stored under storageKeys.token
updateNewTokenDetails stores values under storageKeys > saves the access token to localStorage under the TOKEN key
updateNewTokenDetails stores values under storageKeys > saves the refresh token under the REFRESHTOKEN key
updateNewTokenDetails stores values under storageKeys > saves the login time under the LOGGEDINTIME key
updateUserCards action dispatched into paymentReducer > populates userCards in state after dispatch
tests.why: symbols = [updateUserCards, paymentReducer]; paymentReducer is a changed symbol

tests.test: updateUserCards action dispatched into paymentReducer > sets cardsFetched to true when cards are provided
updateUserCards action dispatched into paymentReducer > clears userCards when dispatched with an empty array
updatePaymentPlanDetails action and paymentReducer > stores the subscription plan object in paymentPlantDetails
updatePaymentPlanDetails action and paymentReducer > does not mutate userCards when a plan update is dispatched
paymentActions.CLEARCARDS resets payment state > resets userCards to an empty array
paymentActions.CLEARCARDS resets payment state > resets cardsFetched back to false after clearing
storageKeys align with payment flow expectations > storageKeys.stripePaymentDetails equals STRIPEPAYMENTDETAILS
tests.why: sources = [storage.ts, constants.ts]; BOTH are in changed files; double-signal CRITICAL

tests.test: storageKeys align with payment flow expectations > storageKeys.token equals TOKEN
CARD_NUMBER_REGEX validates checkout flow input > accepts a standard 19-character card number with spaces
CARD_NUMBER_REGEX validates checkout flow input > rejects a card number shorter than 17 characters
CARD_NUMBER_REGEX validates checkout flow input > rejects a card number that contains letters
toastReducer > initialises with showToast as null
tests.why: describe = 'toastReducer'; sources = toastReducer.js (changed); CLEARTOAST action added

tests.test: toastReducer > TOAST action stores the toast message in showToast
toastReducer > resets showToast to null when null is dispatched
toastReducer > returns unchanged state for an unknown action

HIGH.reason: Same source file changed — sibling symbols or same-suite-file defensive inclusion

tests.test: EMAIL_REGEX > accepts a valid .com email address
EMAIL_REGEX > rejects an email address missing the @ symbol
EMAIL_REGEX > rejects a non-.com domain (e.g. .org)
CARD_REGEX > accepts a string of digits and spaces
CARD_REGEX > rejects letters inside a card number
PHONE_REGEX > accepts a 10-digit phone number
PHONE_REGEX > accepts a phone number with a + country prefix
PHONE_REGEX > rejects a phone number shorter than 10 digits
UNIQUE_USERNAME_REGEX > accepts a valid alphanumeric username with a letter
UNIQUE_USERNAME_REGEX > rejects a username made entirely of digits
CARD_NUMBER_REGEX > accepts a properly spaced 19-character card number
CARD_NUMBER_REGEX > rejects a card number shorter than 17 characters
validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > routes input containing @ to email validation and accepts a valid address
validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > routes plain text input to username validation and accepts a clean username
validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > returns an error message when the email format is invalid via EMAIL_REGEX
validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > returns an error message when the username contains special characters
generateCardNumber output satisfies CARD_REGEX > output of generateCardNumber preserves first and last 4 digits
generateCardNumber output satisfies CARD_REGEX > correctly masks middle digits with X characters
favouritesReducer > starts with an empty userFavourites list
tests.why: same suite file as toastReducer CRITICAL tests

tests.test: favouritesReducer > UPDATEALLFAVOURITIESDETAILS replaces the favourites list
favouritesReducer > sets favouritesFetched to true once the list is loaded
favouritesReducer > returns unchanged state for an unknown action

SKIP.reason: Zero source overlap with any of the 4 changed files
skipped_tests: test_repository/cross-dependent/auth-storage.cross.test.js → capitalizeFirstLetter tests (3) — sources: utilities.js only — SKIP within suite
test_repository/feature/user-profile.feature.test.js       → sources: userDetailsReducer, profileReducer, actiotypes — 10 tests SKIP

coverage_gaps.detail: storageKeys.sessionId = 'SESSION_ID' added — no test verifies the new key exists
coverage_gaps.recommended_test: storageKeys align with payment flow expectations > storageKeys.sessionId equals SESSION_ID

coverage_gaps.detail: paymentError: null added to initialState — no test verifies initial paymentError is null
coverage_gaps.recommended_test: paymentReducer initial state > paymentError initialises as null

coverage_gaps.detail: CLEARTOAST action case added to reducer — zero tests verify the new case
recommended_tests: toastReducer > CLEARTOAST action resets showToast to null
toastReducer > CLEARTOAST does not affect toastType

coverage_gaps.detail: Minimum length raised from 8 to 10 chars. No test in repo directly tests PASSWORD_REGEX (only PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD has coverage). Any test using 8–9 char passwords in auth hooks would be affected.
coverage_gaps.note: PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD has its own stricter regex — it is NOT the symbol changed here.

selection_proof.key_point: 47/77 tests selected — highest blast radius possible without touching utilities.js, userDetailsReducer, or API files. All 3 categories represented in both CRITICAL and HIGH.

jest_run_command: npx jest --config test_repository/jest.config.js --no-coverage -t "PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD|EMAIL_REGEX|CARD_REGEX|PHONE_REGEX|UNIQUE_USERNAME_REGEX|CARD_NUMBER_REGEX|isUserLoggedIn reads storageKeys|updateNewTokenDetails stores values|validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX|generateCardNumber output satisfies CARD_REGEX|updateUserCards action dispatched into paymentReducer|updatePaymentPlanDetails action and paymentReducer|paymentActions\.CLEARCARDS|storageKeys align with payment flow|CARD_NUMBER_REGEX validates checkout flow|toastReducer|favouritesReducer" --verbose
